steps.title: Write standard form
steps.content:
Substitute the completed squares and move constants to the other side.
$$(x+9)^2-81+(y+6)^2-36=-36$$
$$(x+9)^2+(y+6)^2=81$$

steps.title: Read the center
steps.content: Standard form is $(x-h)^2+(y-k)^2=r^2$. Here, $(x+9)^2=(x-(-9))^2$ and $(y+6)^2=(y-(-6))^2$, so the center is $(-9,-6)$. The answer is B.
